meta.note: Faithful, extractive summaries; every item links to its original source.

items.headline: Google justifies its massive AI spending with a booming cloud business
items.summary: Google's cloud business is thriving, as companies adopting its AI and AI infrastructure services help the tech giant to report record profits.

items.headline: Treasury threatens sanctions after White House claims Moonshot distilled Anthropic’s Fable
items.summary: The episode has also intensified a broader debate in Washington over the influx of Chinese open models.

items.headline: How OpenAI’s human mistake led to the AI-powered hack on Hugging Face
items.summary: OpenAI made a mistake setting up what it called a “highly isolated” testing environment and sandbox. According to cybersecurity experts, that human mistake is what made the AI-powered attack on Hugging Face possible.

items.headline: How 'Artificial Intelligence' Is Slowly Drowning in Its Own Droppings
items.summary: 4 points and 0 comments on Hacker News.

items.headline: Travis Kalanick’s robotics company raises $1.7B, led by a16z
items.summary: Uber is also investing in Travis Kalanick's company Atoms, which has made gauzy claims about using industrial AI to modernize the world.

items.headline: Amazon cuts some jobs in its artificial general intelligence unit
items.summary: 2 points and 0 comments on Hacker News.

items.headline: Hyundai claims humanoid robot plan is not part of talks with striking workers
items.summary: Union previously warned automaker that any robot deployment must be negotiated.

items.headline: Yope raises $12.3M to build a private social network without algorithms or ads
items.summary: Yope, a fast-growing social app focused on private groups of friends and family, has raised $12.3 million in seed funding. Instead of chasing creators and algorithmic feeds, the startup is betting that the future of social networking lies…

items.headline: Amazon Cuts Jobs in Artificial General Intelligence Unit
items.summary: 6 points and 3 comments on Hacker News.

items.headline: Monday.com lays off hundreds to focus on AI
items.summary: The company said it is reducing its headcount by 20%, or about 630 staff, to "support a leaner, more focused operating model" as it focuses on its AI Work Platform.

items.headline: OpenAI says its AI agent broke out of testing sandbox to hack Hugging Face
items.summary: "This is day one for cybersecurity in the age of agents," Hugging Face CEO says.

items.headline: Here’s what Samsung’s smart glasses actually look like
items.summary: Samsung has given us our first chance to check out its upcoming smart glasses in person, revealing two new designs and the first specs in the process, including an impressive 9-hour battery life. The glasses, developed in collaboration…

items.headline: Arcee, a US open source AI lab, says Chinese models are not inherently dangerous
items.summary: As Chinese AI models grow in capability and popularity among U.S. companies, the arguing over what should be done about them has reached a fever pitch.

items.headline: Substack’s new tool tells you who’s been writing their newsletters with AI
items.summary: Substack is giving readers a way to estimate how much of a newsletter was written by AI, signaling a broader shift toward transparency around AI-assisted content.

items.headline: OpenAI’s AI spending spree has ballooned to $750B
items.summary: OpenAI will spend the equivalent of Sweden's GDP on infrastructure through 2030.

items.headline: Amazon cuts jobs in its artificial general intelligence group
items.summary: 7 points and 1 comments on Hacker News.

items.headline: AMD commits up to $5 billion to Anthropic
items.summary: AMD says it's going to invest up to $5 billion in Anthropic, while helping to expand the AI company's computing power, according to an announcement on Wednesday. As part of the new partnership, Anthropic will deploy up to 2 gigawatts of…

items.headline: Menlo Ventures’ Matt Murphy explains what AI startups founders must do differently
items.summary: Anthropic leaped to a $47 billion revenue run rate by May, compared to $9 billion in 2025. It’s the kind of growth that Menlo Ventures’ Matt Murphy says he’s never seen in 25 years of investing, not in the internet wave, not in mobile, not…

items.headline: Accelerating the frontiers of scientific discovery: Google’s $40M commitment to the Genesis Mission
items.summary: Google commits $40M in AI tokens and credits for the Genesis Mission

items.headline: Unlimited AI tokens aren't unlimited after all as US Army burns through supply
items.summary: Troops received an email informing them that they were rapidly depleting their AI tokens.

items.headline: The browser wars aren’t about search anymore — here are the best alternatives to Chrome and Safari
items.summary: We’ve compiled an overview of some of the top alternative browsers available today aiming to challenge Chrome and Safari.

items.headline: Passionfroot raises $15M to expand its B2B creator marketplace to the US
items.summary: Passionfroot, a German startup building a marketplace connecting B2B creators with brands, has raised $15M in a Series A round led by Insight Partners.

items.headline: Building AI infrastructure with the Effingham County community
items.summary: OpenAI announces Project Camellia in Effingham County, Georgia, with commitments to responsible energy, community investment, jobs, and access to Codex.

items.headline: How news organizations are using AI to advance their vital missions
items.summary: News organizations are using AI to strengthen reporting, grow audiences, and improve business operations, with OpenAI tools supporting journalists and publishers worldwide.
